title: Naming Rules for Sybase
text: Naming Rules for Sybase Table names Letters, numbers, dollar signs ( $ ) and underscores (_) are allowed. The first character must be a letter. Table names may be between one and 30 characters long. Uniface only accepts uppercase names in the application model definition but creates all lowercase names. Sybase, on the other hand, allows a mixture; you can correctly reference mixed-case table names via assignment tables. Note: If an entity in the application model is called TABLE1 , even if the assignment file refers to it in uppercase as TABLE1 , the connector will create a table with the lowercase name table1 . If the assignment file refers to this table with a name that differs in any way from the model name, including the same name with a mixed case, for example Table1 or TableOne , the connector uses the exact name of the assignment. The names of Sybase stored procedures and triggers are created using the table name as a base. If the table name is longer than 21 characters, the trigger or procedure name is created using only the first 21 characters of the table name. Therefore, table names must be unique in the first 21 characters for all platforms. Field names The same rules apply except that field names cannot be renamed in the assignment file.
